21.correct: The 42nd Constitutional Amendment (1976) is known as the 'Mini Constitution' because of its very wide-ranging changes.

wrong.B: It is not called the 'Federal Amendment'.
wrong.C: It is not the 'Panchayati Raj Amendment' (that was the 73rd).
wrong.D: It is not the 'Fundamental Rights Amendment'.

21.fact: The 42nd Amendment added the words 'Socialist', 'Secular' and 'Integrity' to the Preamble.

22.correct: A sheet of paper falls more slowly than a stone in air mainly because of air resistance acting on its larger surface area.

wrong.A: In a vacuum both would fall together, so mass difference is not the cause.
wrong.C: Shape matters only because it affects air resistance.
wrong.D: Gravity acts equally; the difference is due to air resistance.

22.fact: In a vacuum, a feather and a coin fall at the same rate.

23.correct: At the start of a free fall from a height, the potential energy is maximum (greater than zero) and the kinetic energy is zero (the object is momentarily at rest).

wrong.A: KE is zero at the start, not greater than zero.
wrong.C: This option is garbled but PE is maximum, KE is zero.
wrong.D: KE is not greater than zero at the very start.

23.fact: As the object falls, PE converts into KE, conserving total mechanical energy.

24.correct: We always see the same face of the Moon because it rotates on its axis in the same time it takes to revolve around the Earth (tidal locking).

wrong.A: The Moon does rotate; it is not non-rotating.
wrong.C: Earth's rotation does not cancel the Moon's rotation.
wrong.D: The Moon is tidally locked to the Earth, not the Sun.

24.fact: This synchronous rotation means the 'far side' of the Moon is never visible from Earth.

25.correct: ISRO's IRNSS (NavIC) system is meant for regional navigation - providing positioning over India and its surrounding region.

wrong.A: It is not a weather-forecasting system.
wrong.B: It is not an Earth-imaging system.
wrong.D: It is not a communication system.

25.fact: NavIC (Navigation with Indian Constellation) is India's indigenous satellite navigation system.

26.correct: The Gulf War and the resulting rise in oil prices worsened India's balance-of-payments crisis in the early 1990s.

wrong.A: The Asian financial crisis came later (1997).
wrong.C: The collapse of the USSR contributed but the immediate trigger was the Gulf War oil shock.
wrong.D: A fall in agricultural exports was not the main external cause.

26.fact: The 1991 crisis forced India to pledge gold and adopt sweeping economic reforms.

27.correct: A standard women's kabaddi match has two halves of 15 minutes each.

wrong.A: 20 minutes per half is the men's format.

27.fact: Men's kabaddi matches are two halves of 20 minutes each.

28.correct: The Betwa is a tributary of the Yamuna, NOT directly of the Ganga (the others - Son, Gandak, Ghaghara - are Ganga tributaries).

wrong.A: The Son is a tributary of the Ganga.
wrong.B: The Gandak is a tributary of the Ganga.
wrong.C: The Ghaghara is a tributary of the Ganga.

28.fact: The Betwa joins the Yamuna, which in turn joins the Ganga at Prayagraj.

29.correct: In India, the Green Revolution refers mainly to a huge increase in food-grain production (especially wheat and rice).

wrong.B: It was not about increasing green (forest) cover.
wrong.C: It was not chiefly about horticulture.

29.fact: The Green Revolution used HYV seeds, fertilizers, irrigation and machinery.

30.correct: Guru Gobind Singh founded the Khalsa Panth in 1699 (on Vaisakhi).

wrong.A: 1606 is when Guru Arjan Dev was martyred.

30.fact: The Khalsa was founded at Anandpur Sahib with the initiation of the Panj Pyare.

31.correct: In July 2025, the Andhra Pradesh government launched the 'Digi-Lakshmi' scheme to digitally empower urban poor women.

wrong.B: Tamil Nadu did not launch it.

31.fact: Such schemes aim to improve women's access to digital and financial services.

32.correct: Both ocean currents (I) and relief (topographic) features (II) control the climate of a place, so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both influence climate.

32.fact: Other climate controls include latitude, altitude, distance from the sea and winds.

33.correct: The SHRESHTHA scheme covers both residential schools (I) and, broadly, support for students in non-residential settings (II) - so 'Both I and II'.
33.fact: SHRESHTHA supports meritorious Scheduled Caste students' education in quality schools.

34.correct: Article 343 of the Constitution deals with the official language of the Union (Hindi in Devanagari script).

wrong.C: Article 344 deals with the official-language commission/committee.
wrong.D: Article 339 deals with the administration of Scheduled Areas/Tribes.

34.fact: Article 343 also provided for the continued use of English for official purposes.

35.correct: Article 213 deals with the power of the Governor to promulgate ordinances when the legislature is not in session.

wrong.B: Article 211 restricts discussion in the legislature about judges' conduct.
wrong.C: Article 210 deals with the language used in the legislature.
wrong.D: Article 208 deals with rules of procedure.

35.fact: The President's equivalent ordinance power is under Article 123.

36.correct: The classical dance form 'Kathakali' belongs to Kerala.
36.fact: Kathakali is known for elaborate make-up, costumes and storytelling from the epics.

37.correct: 'Krishnanattam', a traditional art depicting the life of Lord Krishna, originated in Kerala.

wrong.C: It is not from Tamil Nadu.

37.fact: Krishnanattam is performed mainly at the Guruvayur temple in Kerala.

38.correct: The traditional Rajasthani instrument that is a long bronze trumpet made in two parts with a flared (funnel-shaped) mouth is the 'Turhi' (Turahi).

wrong.B: The Timki is a small drum.
wrong.C: The Sarod is a string instrument.
wrong.D: The Santoor is a struck string instrument.

38.fact: The Turhi is a ceremonial trumpet used in folk processions in Rajasthan.

39.correct: Kabaddi is mainly known as 'Hu-Tu-Tu' in Western India (Maharashtra region).

wrong.B: In the east it has other names.
wrong.C: In the south it is called Chedugudu.
wrong.D: In the north it has different local names.

39.fact: Kabaddi is known by various regional names across India.

40.correct: Among the options, weightlifting has not yet won India a gold medal at the Asian Games.

wrong.A: Shooting has won India Asian Games gold.
wrong.B: Golf has won India an Asian Games gold.
wrong.D: Athletics has won India Asian Games gold.

40.fact: India has won many Asian Games golds in athletics, shooting and other sports.
